name: guardian-encryption-skill
description: Apply GUARDIAN encryption to completed codebases - makes code immutable and enforces execution
instructions:
When the user asks to set up Guardian Encryption, follow these steps:

1. EXPLAIN what Guardian Encryption does:
   - Makes code IMMUTABLE (cannot be modified without approval)
   - Enforces code execution (cannot be ignored by agents)
   - Requires natural language approval for changes
   - Scans for API keys before encrypting

2. ASK which directory to encrypt:
   - Default: current working directory
   - Or ask: 'Which codebase should I encrypt?'

3. RUN the encryption:
   ```bash
   guardian-encrypt.sh <directory>
   ```

4. VERIFY encryption was applied:
   - Check files have immutable flag
   - Confirm GUARDIAN_ENCRYPTED.md was created
   - Show user the protection status

5. DOCUMENT what was encrypted:
   - List all protected files
   - Explain how to request changes
   - Show approval workflow

IMPORTANT:
- Only encrypt COMPLETED codebases (not WIP)
- API key scanner runs automatically
- If keys found, encryption is BLOCKED until fixed
- Encrypted code CANNOT be modified without human approval

commands.name: guardian-encrypt
commands.description: Apply GUARDIAN encryption to a codebase
